messages.content:
# Prompt: spec-draft.system

version: 0.1.0
target artifact: AIL-Spec Canonical

## Purpose

Convert checked AIL-Requirements into canonical structured English accepted by
the parser.

## Input Schema

```json
{
  "requirements": "",
  "profile": "",
  "package_manifest": "",
  "required_features": []
}
```

## Output Schema

Use the common prompt envelope with `artifact_kind` set to
`AIL-Spec Canonical`.

## Forbidden Behavior

- Do not use friendly paraphrase where canonical grammar is required.
- Do not add default failures, approvals, traces, or external calls without
  provenance.
- Do not suppress unresolved questions.

## Provenance And Handoff

Each canonical section includes provenance back to requirements. The generated
spec is handed to the parser and checker.

## Valid Example

```text
Action: Close ticket.

When a support agent closes a ticket:

- the system requires the ticket to exist
- the system changes the ticket status to Closed
- the system records a trace event named TicketClosed
```

## Invalid Example

```text
The app should probably close tickets normally.
```

Reason: not canonical, missing actor, writes, and trace.

## Round-Trip Expectation

AIL-Spec Canonical renders to AIL-Core and back without semantic hash drift.

TASK:
Return only complete canonical AIL-Spec text.

Use these exact section forms:
- The application Support Tickets manages ...
- The application has these users:
- A User has:
- A Ticket has:
- The application shows:
- Action: Create ticket.
- When a customer creates a ticket:
- bullet lines beginning "- the system ..."
- Failure NotFound happens when ...:

Preserve field type annotations exactly, including State<...>, Option<User>,
Time, List<Text>, and Secret<List<Text>>. For action traces use "the system
records a trace event named X". For failure traces use "the trace records X".
Use "the ticket status" when referring to ticket status.

Do not use markdown fences, commentary, "Users:", "Ticket fields:", "Actions:",
or "Failures:" headings.

INPUT JSON:
{
  "package_manifest": "# AIL Package\n\nname: support-ticket\nversion: 0.1.0\nprofile: Application\nentry: spec.ail-spec.md\nfeatures: things, actions, failures, guarantees, traces, secrets\nconformance: first-slice\ntarget-support:\n  linux-x86_64-elf: supported\n  wasm32-unknown-sandbox-wasm: supported-with-host-imports\n  aarch64-apple-darwin-libsystem-macho: planned-contract\n",
  "profile": "Application",
  "required_features": [
    "things",
    "actions",
    "failures",
    "guarantees",
    "traces",
    "secrets"
  ],
  "requirements": "AIL-Requirements:\n- The application Support Tickets manages customer support tickets, assignments, updates, internal notes, and overdue-ticket review.\n- The application users are Customer, Support agent, and Support manager.\n- A User has fields id: Text, role: State<Customer, SupportAgent, SupportManager>, and email: Text.\n- A Ticket has fields id: Text, title: Text, status: State<New, Open, Assigned, Closed, Overdue>, customer: User, assignee: Option<User>, created_at: Time, due_at: Time, public_updates: List<Text>, and internal notes: Secret<List<Text>>.\n- The application shows an open ticket queue for support agents.\n- The application shows an Overdue tickets view for support managers.\n- The application shows a customer-visible ticket history that includes public updates and never includes internal notes.\n- The Create ticket action is performed by a customer.\n- Create ticket requires the customer id and title.\n- Create ticket creates a Ticket with status New, records the customer as the ticket customer, records an initial public update, guarantees internal notes are empty and secret, and records a trace event named TicketCreated.\n- The Assign ticket action is performed by a support agent.\n- Assign ticket requires the ticket to exist, requires the ticket status to be New or Open, and requires the assignee role to be SupportAgent or SupportManager.\n- Assign ticket changes the ticket assignee, changes the status to Assigned, records a public update, guarantees the assignee can see internal notes, and records a trace event named TicketAssigned.\n- The Close ticket action is performed by a support agent.\n- Close ticket requires the ticket to exist and requires the ticket status not to be Closed.\n- Close ticket changes the ticket status to Closed, records a public update, does not reveal internal notes to the customer, guarantees closed tickets do not appear in the open ticket queue, and records a trace event named TicketClosed.\n- The scheduler marks overdue tickets by reading tickets whose status is New, Open, or Assigned.\n- Marking overdue tickets requires the current time to be later than due_at, changes the ticket status to Overdue, records a public update, and records a trace event named TicketOverdue.\n- Failure NotFound happens when a ticket id does not match a stored ticket, changes no ticket data, shows Ticket not found, and the trace records TicketNotFound.\n- Failure PermissionDenied happens when a user tries to see internal notes without support staff permission, reveals no secret value, shows Permission denied, and the trace records InternalNotesDenied.\n"
}
